name: Indian Mackerel
description: A common, medium-sized mackerel that swims in schools and is important for both local consumption and commercial fisheries.
location: Manila Bay, West Philippine Sea (off the coast of Central Luzon)

name: Tilapia
description: A widely farmed freshwater fish known for being a low-cost protein source in local communities.

name: Short-bodied Mackerel
description: A medium-sized tuna species that's commercially important in the region. Often caught in coastal waters and sold in wet markets. Also known as Skipjack Tuna.
location: Lagonoy Gulf, Manila Bay, Bohol, Celebes Sea

name: Tamban
description: Small, oily, schooling fish that are essential to marine food webs and heavily harvested for commercial use.
location: West Philippine Sea (off the coast of Central Luzon), Manila Bay

name: Squid
description: Cephalopods with elongated bodies, large eyes, and ten arms. A popular seafood enjoyed grilled, fried, or stuffed.
location: West Philippine Sea (off the coast of Central Luzon), Manila Bay

name: Anchovy
description: Small, schooling fish with a salty and umami flavor. They are often used in sauces, pastes, or eaten whole. Also known as Bolinao.
location: Leyte, Samar, Iligan Bay, Manila Bay

name: Bangus
description: The national fish of the Philippines and the most important cultured species in the country. Pampanga and Bulacan are major producers.

name: Yellowtail Fusilier
description: Known for its vibrant colors and delicate flavor, often sold in wet markets in Metro Manila and cooked in sinigang or fried.

name: Dilis
description: Small, schooling fish found near the coast, often used for fish paste and dried fish products.
location: Coastal waters of Cavite, Batangas, and Quezon, including Manila Bay.
